text:
Frank Lloyd Wright’s decision to use cantilevered, reinforced concrete balconies in his design 
of Fallingwater allowed him to

options.A:
divide the interior of the house into distinctly separate spaces dedicated to specific 
functions
options.B:
create a dynamic spatial interplay between the interior of the house and the surrounding 
landscape
options.C: dominate the surrounding landscape through the creation of monumental forms
options.D:
ensure the structural integrity of the house in an area known for its seismic activity
Answer: B
Content Area
Learning Objective

text:
The location of the Lanzón Stela in the Old Temple of Chavín de Huántar suggests that the 
creators of the building most likely intended to

options.A: facilitate pilgrims’ circumambulation of the stela during annual processions
options.B: heighten the spiritual drama of the viewer’s ritualized encounter with the stela
options.C: maximize the stela’s exposure to the rising sun on the winter solstice
options.D:
enhance visibility of the stela for the audiences during coronation
Answer: B
Content Area
Learning Objective

Minimalism

depicting Athens' triumph over Rome in

The use of over one million small tiles to make
the Alexander Mosaic allowed its creator to

options.A:
depict a disorganized scene that
overwhelms the viewer
options.B:
model the anatomy of the figures
naturalistically
options.C:
modify and transport the composition
easily
options.D: reduce the figures into abstract forms

text: Originally located in a private house in Pompeii, the Alexander Mosaic was likely interpreted by viewers in which of the following ways?

options.A: It conveyed the elevated and learned status of the owner of the house.
options.B: It exemplified the fears of those who lived near an active volcano.
options.C: It underscored the political aspirations of the elite class.
options.D: It functioned as a memorial to the owner’s heroic deeds.

text: The foreshortening of figures in the Alexander Mosaic likely affected viewers in which of the following ways?

options.A: It reminded viewers of the chaos of everyday life.
options.B: It conveyed the climactic moment of a narrative.
options.C: It resulted in a detached scene that appeared to occur in the past.
options.D: It divided the narrative into a sequence of scenes.

text: The precise arrangement and orientation of the megaliths at Stonehenge suggest that the site was used for the

options.B: calculation of the positions of the planets
options.C: performance of a ceremony dedicated to a lunar deity
options.D: commemoration of the creation of the Heavens

text: The mortuary temple of Hatshepsut differs from the Great Pyramids at Giza in that the temple did not function as a

options.C: symbol of political and religious power

text:
The Parthenon and the White Temple at Uruk 
both included which of the following 
architectural features?

options.A:
A sacred cella that contained votive figures 
or cult statues
options.B:
A peripteral colonnade that housed an 
open-air market
options.C:
A polished marble exterior that reflected 
the sun’s divine light
options.D:
A processional staircase that restricted 
access to the inner sanctum

text:
Which of the following characteristics of 
Jean-Antoine Houdon’s sculpture of George 
Washington made it most appropriate for display 
in the rotunda of a government building?

options.A:
Its pose, which mimics the dramatic stance 
of the Winged Victory of Samothrace
options.B:
Its uniform, which recalls the military 
attire of Augustus of Prima Porta
options.C:
Its use of Neoclassical imagery, which 
evokes the political ideals of ancient 
Rome
options.D:
Its use of Greek idealism, which conveys 
Washington’s godlike stature as a leader

traditions because Vigée Le Brun

was among the first women to depict

options.B:
used her self-portrait to convey coded 
political ideas
options.C:
rejected the inclusion of religious 
references in her self portrait
options.D:
depicted herself standing instead of seated 
as would be traditional for women

text:
Jaune Quick-to-See Smith made Trade (Gifts for 
Trading Land with White People) partly in 
reaction to which of the following events?

options.A:
The passage of the Water Rights Protection 
Act that preserved rivers on Native 
American lands
options.B:
The expiration of the federal government’s 
termination policy that broke up the 
reservation system
options.C:
The 500-year anniversary of Christopher 
Columbus’s arrival in the Americas
options.D:
The emergence of the Red Power 
movement among Native Americans 
during the Civil Rights era

text:
Quick-to-See Smith’s inclusion of cheap trinkets 
is intended to

options.A:
emphasize that most popular American 
sports are variations of Native American 
traditions
options.B:
reference a history of exploitative 
economic transactions between Native 
Americans and Europeans
options.C:
expose the low wages of Native American 
workers who labor in factories to 
mass-produce such goods
options.D:
critique the art market’s obsession with the 
skill and craftsmanship of American art

AP Art History Practice Exam 
12

text:
Quick-to-See Smith chose to use collaged 
materials from commercial products and popular 
culture both to highlight contemporary racial 
stereotypes and to

options.A:
create patterns that imitated Native 
American beadwork
options.B:
raise ecological awareness through the 
reuse of materials
options.C:
challenge the gestural processes of 
mid-twentieth-century American 
abstractionists
options.D:
demonstrate that Native American artists 
were engaged in contemporary art 
practice

options.A:
comment satirically on the arrogance of the

options.B:
appeal to the civic pride of the Florentine

options.C:
represent divine approval of the king and

options.D:

text:
Diego Velázquez’ Las Meninas, though 
influenced by Rubens’ royal portraits, differs 
from them in which of the following ways?

options.A:
Velazquez depicted royalty as solemn and 
spiritual.
options.B:
Velazquez created a dense allegorical 
narrative to portray royalty.
options.C:
Velazquez provided a formal, idealized 
portrayal of the royal family.
options.D:
Velazquez showed the royal family in 
candid aspects of daily life.

text:
Marie de’ Medici, commissioned this work, as 
well as the larger cycle it belongs to, in order to

options.A:
appease foreign governments during a time 
of war in France
options.B:
support her husband’s rule and defend his 
unpopular decisions
options.C:
celebrate her virtues and advocate her 
political position
options.D:
justify France’s involvement in European 
military conflicts of the time
